title: Gigantochloa atter
taxonomy: Gigantochloa atter is classified as a species of bamboo in the genus Gigantochloa. This genus belongs to the family Poaceae, which consists of various grasses. Gigantochloa atter is native to Southeast Asia and is known for its tall, woody stems. The species is often found in tropical rainforests and is considered an important component of the ecosystem. As a member of the Poaceae family, Gigantochloa atter is a type of monocot, characterized by its single cotyledon in the seedling stage.
